title: allium bolanderi var. stenanthum
intro: Allium bolanderi var. stenanthum, also known as Bolander's onion, is a perennial plant belonging to the Allium genus, which includes various species of wild onions and garlic. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, acting as an antibacterial agent, and possessing antioxidant properties. Bolander's onion can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
